_comment: Catalog-driven pixel-OBJECT factory. An 'object' is one prop/tool/item (chest, coin, rock, bird, sword, shovel, tree...). Each object is a self-contained folder under objects/<id>/. The loop reads the filesystem and, per object, generates: (1) a base sprite (generate-image-pixflux), (2) optional rotated views (rotate), (3) optional animations (animate-with-text). Every unit = one PixelLab generation; after each it rebuilds the viewer, commits and pushes. Fully resumable. Unlike characters, objects are stateless image tools with no server-side object to sync back — the repo is the source of truth.
style_base: detailed RPG pixel art inspired by Grave Seasons and Stardew Valley, clean readable silhouette, naturalistic lighting and a warm lifelike palette, soft shading, crisp pixels, single centered game asset on a transparent background

defaults.negative_description: blurry, multiple objects, duplicated, cropped, text, watermark, drop shadow, background scenery, tiling

scale._comment: REALISM RULE: objects are scaled to the world by real height, so they sit correctly next to characters. A character is ~character_height_px tall (the 64px skeleton) representing character_height_m. The loop turns each object's world_height_m into world_px_height = round(world_height_m * character_height_px / character_height_m); a game renders the sprite scaled to that pixel height. So a coin (~0.22m) is tiny next to a character and an oak (~6m) towers. Every object MUST have a world_height_m (per-object, else category_height_m fallback).

budget._comment: Shared PixelLab pool floor (see coordination/PROTOCOL.md). The loop stops cleanly when the balance drops below this, leaving headroom for the characters and maps domains that draw from the same pool.

targets._comment: How many objects the loop aims to produce. It builds the explicit `catalog` first, then synthesizes further ones from `procedural` until this count is reached. Raise it to keep the loop generating open-endedly.

rotation_directions._comment: A rotated object keeps its base sprite as `south` and rotates into the rest. Base is always generated at `defaults.direction` (south).

_animation_library: Reusable animation definitions, referenced by key from an object's `animations` list (a bare string uses the library entry as-is; an object {key, action, n_frames, view} overrides fields). `action` is kept to ONE simple verb/phrase — extra words confuse PixelLab. animate-with-text needs a canvas >= 64, so any animated object is generated at >= 64x64.

glint.action: glinting with a shine of light

_catalog: Explicit, curated objects. Each is one unique folder objects/<id>/. `size` is [w,h]; `rotations` is 0 (none), 4, or 8; `animations` lists animation_library keys (or inline overrides). Objects with animations are auto-bumped to >= 64x64.

_procedural: When the explicit catalog is exhausted but targets.num_objects is not yet reached, the loop synthesizes more objects by combining these axes. Deterministic (seeded by index) so re-runs are reproducible. These are best-effort, un-curated extras.
